headline_summary: Iran-linked attacks and conditional transit policies keep shipping risk elevated in the Strait of Hormuz, sustaining an oil risk premium.
Escalation expanded from maritime disruption into direct strikes and knock-on outages across Gulf refining, LNG and petrochemical assets (Saudi Arabia, Kuwait, Bahrain, UAE, Qatar, Iraq).
U.S. strikes at/near Iran’s Kharg Island export hub and threats of wider retaliation keep tail-risk of prolonged supply losses high.
Selective/neutral transits (e.g., a French-owned CMA CGM vessel) show passage is possible but politicized, keeping freight and insurance volatile.
OPEC+ is discussing/incrementing quotas, but officials and analysts emphasize additional supply is largely academic while Hormuz remains constrained.
risk_drivers: Strait of Hormuz throughput remains constrained/conditional; attack and mining threats persist
Damage to major Gulf refining/LNG/petrochemical assets and slow recovery timelines
Direct attacks on export terminals and storage/processing hubs (Kharg Island, Ras Laffan, Sitra)
Force majeure and rerouting raise freight/war-risk insurance and reduce effective supply
Escalation ladder (ultimatums/retaliation cycles) driving headline volatility and risk-off flows

Energy: oil_up_risk: geopolitical premium persists while Hormuz is constrained and Gulf facilities face drone/missile risk
Higher war-risk insurance/freight and episodic force majeure can tighten physical supply beyond headline production
LNG outages (Ras Laffan) increase gas price volatility and may pull incremental oil demand via switching
FX: em_fx_pressure: energy-importer EM FX vulnerable to higher oil/LNG bills and wider current-account deficits
risk_off_flows: USD/JPY/CHF tend to bid on escalation; pressure on high-beta and frontier FX
Rates: rates_credit_spreads: wider EM/HY spreads on risk-off plus fiscal stress for energy importers
Oil-driven inflation risk can lift breakevens even as growth expectations soften
Equities: risk_off_flows: transport/airlines/consumer cyclicals vulnerable; relative support for energy/defense
GCC equities: volatility tied to outage headlines, fiscal offsets from oil, and security posture

sources.title: How the US-Israeli war with Iran is disrupting oil and gas
Kuwait Petroleum Corp reports damage at units after Iran drone attack
OPEC+ agrees to boost oil output when Strait of Hormuz reopens
UAE says the use of Hormuz must be guaranteed in any US-Iran deal
French-owned CMA CGM container ship passes Strait of Hormuz, data shows
Three workers wounded in drone strike on Iraq's North Rumaila oilfield, sources say
Bahrain's Gulf Petrochemical Industries says fire under control at units after Iran drone attack
Qatar LNG, Saudi refinery, Israeli oil, gas fields down due to Mideast strikes
Iran says Iraqi ships can pass Strait of Hormuz as transits tick up

events.title: U.S. forces struck targets at/near Iran’s Kharg Island oil export terminal
events.implication: Raises risk of sustained disruption to Iranian exports and retaliation across Gulf energy infrastructure; adds to oil risk premium.

events.title: Iran says it attacked Saudi Arabia’s Jubail petrochemical complex
events.implication: Deepens regional energy-infrastructure risk; supports higher refined product and petrochemical disruption premia.

events.title: Shipping through the Strait of Hormuz nearly halted after Iran declared it closed
events.implication: A near-stop in transits at the key oil/LNG chokepoint increases delivered-cost shocks via insurance and freight, sustaining elevated crude volatility.

events.title: Iran threatened to mine the Strait of Hormuz if its southern coast or islands are attacked
events.implication: Mining threat materially raises tail-risk of complete stoppage and higher war-risk insurance, sustaining elevated crude risk premium.

events.title: Iran warned allies could shut the Bab el-Mandeb Strait, an alternative route for Saudi exports
events.implication: Threatens to widen shipping disruption beyond Hormuz into the Red Sea route, amplifying global trade and energy delivery risks.

events.title: Iran barred ships linked to the U.S., Israel and their allies while allowing some nationalities to pass
events.implication: Selective interdiction regime keeps logistics uncertain and politicizes route access; raises insurance and delays even for permitted shippers.

events.title: French-owned CMA CGM container ship 'Kribi' crossed the Strait of Hormuz using AIS 'Owner France' destination
events.implication: Shows selective/neutral-flag tactics can enable limited transits; implies politicized passage and continued volatility for shipping and insurance.

events.title: Iran says Iraqi ships can pass the Strait of Hormuz as transits tick up
events.implication: Signals conditional easing for select flags; markets likely to treat this as fragile and reversible, keeping freight and price volatility elevated.

events.title: Iranian drone attacks caused fires and severe damage at Kuwait Petroleum Corp operating units
events.implication: Kuwait refining and export disruptions tighten regional product markets; long repair timelines increase sustained supply risk.

events.title: Kuwait said units at Mina Al-Ahmadi and Mina Abdullah refineries were hit; full recovery could take months
events.implication: Extended outage risk lifts refined-product premia and increases dependency on imports; supportive of higher cracks and freight.

events.title: Bahrain’s Bapco Energies declared force majeure after an attack on the Sitra refinery
events.implication: Refinery force majeure signals material operational disruption; increases regional product tightness and risk premia.

events.title: Bahrain said an Iranian strike set a storage tank on fire
events.implication: Adds to refined product supply risk and raises probability of longer force majeure at the Sitra refinery.

events.title: Bahrain GPIC said fire was brought under control after an Iranian drone attack
events.implication: Adds to petrochemicals/fertiliser supply risk and underscores Bahrain industrial-asset vulnerability amid drone attacks.

events.title: Saudi Arabia suspended operations at the Ras Tanura refinery amid attacks
events.implication: Shuts significant refining capacity and disrupts export logistics; supportive of higher oil/product prices and risk-off sentiment.

events.title: Two drones were intercepted at Ras Tanura and debris caused a limited fire; some units shut as a precaution
events.implication: Even contained incidents can trigger precautionary shutdowns, sustaining outage risk premia and operational uncertainty.

events.title: Two drones hit BP-operated North Rumaila oilfield, wounding workers
events.implication: Adds security risk to southern Iraq output and related export logistics; increases perceived vulnerability of oilfield operations.

events.title: Drone hit storage facilities near Basra used by foreign oil companies, sources said
events.implication: Shows attacks extending to logistics and equipment storage; can increase downtime risk even when production is not immediately hit.

events.title: Qatar declared force majeure on LNG shipments amid conflict disruptions
events.implication: Directly tightens global LNG supply, raising gas prices and potentially lifting oil-indexed LNG contract effects.

events.title: Iranian drone attacks hit Qatar’s Ras Laffan LNG complex prompting shutdown/force majeure preparations
events.implication: LNG export interruptions compound energy crisis and can spill into power prices and industrial demand curtailments.

events.title: Drones hit Qatar’s Mesaieed industrial zone amid retaliatory strikes
events.implication: Industrial disruptions can hit petrochemical output and logistics; increases regional risk premia.

events.title: Shell’s Pearl gas-to-liquids plant at Ras Laffan was shut
events.implication: Reduces supply of liquids/condensates and underscores LNG complex fragility amid attacks.

events.title: Israel instructed Chevron to temporarily shut the Leviathan gas field
events.implication: Cuts regional gas exports and can raise LNG demand elsewhere; supports higher energy prices and risk-off.

events.title: Energean shut production serving smaller Israeli gas fields as conflict widened
events.implication: Further reduces regional gas supply; can tighten Eastern Med balances and lift European gas sensitivity.

events.title: Oil production in Iraqi Kurdistan largely halted as operators stopped output as a precaution
events.implication: Adds to supply disruption and increases reliance on southern Iraq exports that face drone and shipping risks.

events.title: Israel attacked parts of Iran’s South Pars gas field and the Asaluyeh processing hub
events.implication: Potentially constrains Iranian gas processing and raises risk of spillover to regional gas/LNG balances.

events.title: Habshan gas processing complex shut after damage from intercepted missile debris
events.implication: Highlights missile/drone interception debris risk to critical gas infrastructure; supports regional supply uncertainty.

events.title: Further interception debris in the UAE killed one person and damaged facilities again
events.implication: Civilian and infrastructure damage can prompt tighter air-defense posture and renewed risk-off market reaction.

events.title: IAEA confirmed premises of Iran’s Bushehr nuclear power plant were struck
events.implication: Elevates tail-risk of broader escalation and regulatory/security responses; contributes to risk-off sentiment.

events.title: OPEC+ agreed to raise May output quotas by 206,000 bpd, while noting impact is limited while Hormuz remains constrained
events.implication: Signals policy readiness to add barrels if shipping normalizes; near-term pricing remains dominated by physical transit and repair constraints.

events.title: UAE said any U.S.-Iran settlement must guarantee access through the Strait of Hormuz
events.implication: Reinforces that freedom of navigation is central to diplomacy; raises probability of expanded U.S.-led maritime security arrangements.

links.from: Shipping through the Strait of Hormuz nearly halted after Iran declared it closed
links.why: Reduced throughput at a chokepoint handling ~20% of global oil/LNG flows forces rerouting and raises supply disruption risk premia.

links.from: Iran threatened to mine the Strait of Hormuz if its southern coast or islands are attacked
links.why: Mining threat raises tail-risk of a complete stoppage, increasing hedging demand and war-risk insurance costs.

links.from: Iran warned allies could shut the Bab el-Mandeb Strait, an alternative route for Saudi exports
links.why: A second maritime chokepoint threat broadens global trade disruption risk, typically boosting safe-haven flows.

links.from: U.S. forces struck targets at/near Iran’s Kharg Island oil export terminal
links.why: Direct strikes on a major export hub increase escalation odds and raise uncertainty across energy and risk assets.

links.from: Iranian drone attacks caused fires and severe damage at Kuwait Petroleum Corp operating units
links.why: Extended refinery/outage risk can raise inflation expectations and tighten financial conditions, widening risk spreads.

links.from: Qatar declared force majeure on LNG shipments amid conflict disruptions
links.why: LNG shortages can spill into broader energy price complex and reinforce risk premia across hydrocarbons.
Higher crude/LNG import bills weaken terms of trade for energy importers, pressuring EM FX and current accounts.

links.from: OPEC+ agreed to raise May output quotas by 206,000 bpd, while noting impact is limited while Hormuz remains constrained
links.why: Reuters-reported comments and analyst framing indicate paper quota increases do little to relieve physical constraints while Hormuz is disrupted.

links.from: UAE said any U.S.-Iran settlement must guarantee access through the Strait of Hormuz
links.to: Shipping through the Strait of Hormuz nearly halted after Iran declared it closed
links.why: The UAE position explicitly centers restoring navigation, linking diplomatic demands to the shipping shutdown.

links.from: French-owned CMA CGM container ship 'Kribi' crossed the Strait of Hormuz using AIS 'Owner France' destination
links.to: Iran barred ships linked to the U.S., Israel and their allies while allowing some nationalities to pass
links.why: Reuters described the ship’s neutrality-signaling tactic and selective passage, consistent with conditional transit rules.
